---
title: "DEEP: Future Fit"
author: "by Jess"
format:
    revealjs:
        theme: [solarized, custom.scss]
        slideNumber: true
        show-slide-number: all
        #reference-location: document
        #logo: images/logo.png
        footer: "https://deep-future-fit.netlify.app/"
        chalkboard:
            theme: whiteboard
        #code-annotations: hover
        css: styles.css
        mermaid:
          theme: default
        highlight-style: github
        #highlight-style: monochrome
        #logo: qr-code.png
        #multiplex: true
editor:
    render-on-save: true

---
# Review

::: {.notes}
blooket + discussion
:::

# Systems Programming

## Systems

- Operating Systems
- Embedded Systems
- Microcontrollers

## Computer Hardware

Every computer has a central processing unit (CPU) that executes instructions.

Types of CPU architectures:

- ARM (Raspberry Pi, Apple Silicon, Qualcomm Snapdragon)
- x86 (Intel/AMD CPUs)
- RISC-V

## Bluetooth Low-Energy (BLE)
Wireless communication designed for low-power, short-range communication.

What applications or use cases is BLE good for?

::: {.fragment .fade-in}
- Fitness trackers
- Healthcare devices
- Smart sensors
- Internet of Things (IoT) devices
:::

::: {.notes}
compare with bluetooth
:::

## Modes

Advertising: the device broadcasts its presence to nearby devices.

<br>

Connection: Bidirectional communication between central and peripheral devices.

## Services and Characteristics

Labels to organize BLE communication:

- Services: a group of related characteristics that provide a specific functionality.
- Characteristics: a single value that can be read or written by the central device.

## Profiles
BLE includes standard profiles that simplify development.

For example:

- Battery Service (0x180F)
    - Battery Level Characteristic (0x2A19)
- Heart Rate Service (0x180D)
    - Heart Rate Measurement Characteristic (0x2A37)

## Arduino


## Circuits
Closed loop flow of electrons

## Programming an Arduino

## Number Representations

::: {.notes}
hand out worksheet
:::

# Javascript

## Functions
- arrow definition

## Callback Functions

## Array Functions

## Variables
- no need to define the type
- let vs const

## Printing to the Screen

## Operations
- three equals signs

## Strings?
